english: Authentic Tea House Ayataka Green Tea

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301103 covers green tea beverage broadly; no specific entry for Ayataka canned RTD product. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers ready-to-drink green tea; no brand-specific Ayataka code exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for canned ready-to-drink green tea beverage product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 300ml can of Ayataka green tea, equivalent to approximately 300g, as sold in Singapore convenience stores and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, green, ready-to-drink, unsweetened
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for unsweetened ready-to-drink green tea (FDC ID 171917) used as the primary nutrient reference. Ayataka is a Japanese RTD green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan; the product is brewed from Gyokuro-style leaves and is unsweetened, making the USDA unsweetened green tea RTD entry the closest defensible match. Per-100g values are derived directly from the USDA entry. Serving size set to 300g (one 300ml can).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — plain tea and low-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ayataka canned green tea. Plain unsweetened green tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates (0.2g per 100g) and has an effectively zero glycemic response. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care), plain tea is classified as GI < 1 and is assigned GI = 1 as the lowest valid positive sentinel value. gi_category set to 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.0 because available carbohydrate per 300ml serving is 0.6g, which rounds to 0.0 GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (1mg per 100g; 3mg per 300ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension; its sodium content is negligible at approximately 3mg per 300ml can, and regular green tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Effectively zero glycemic load; no added sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea has a glycemic load of 0.0 per 300ml serving and contains no added sugars, making it an appropriate beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; catechins in green tea may also support insulin sensitivity per clinical literature.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of green tea catechins (EGCG)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides green tea catechins including EGCG, which have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in peer-reviewed cardiovascular research.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka Canned Green Tea (300ml) is a Japanese ready-to-drink (RTD) green tea beverage produced by Coca-Cola Japan under the 'Authentic Tea House' (本格茶屋) brand. It is brewed using Gyokuro-style green tea leaves, which are shade-grown to enhance umami and reduce bitterness, giving the beverage a smooth, mellow, and slightly grassy flavour profile with a clean finish. The product is unsweetened and contains no added sugars, artificial flavours, or preservatives. Each 300ml can contains approximately 3 kcal, 0.6g of carbohydrate, and negligible protein and fat, making it one of the lowest-calorie packaged beverages available. The sodium content is approximately 3mg per can, which is clinically insignificant. Ayataka is widely distributed across Asia, including Singapore, where it is sold in convenience stores and through food delivery platforms such as field survey. It is served chilled and is popular as a refreshing accompaniment to meals, particularly at Japanese-influenced fast food outlets. From a clinical nutrition perspective, Ayataka unsweetened green tea is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible caloric and glycemic contribution. Green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), present in the brewed tea, have been associated with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective effects in peer-reviewed literature. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Ayataka can?
Is Ayataka green tea suitable for diabetics?
What is Authentic Tea House Ayataka?
Can I drink Ayataka green tea if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of Ayataka canned green tea?

confidence_reason: Ayataka is a commercially produced RTD green tea. No brand-specific nutritional panel was retrievable from an approved primary source at time of processing. USDA SR Legacy entry for unsweetened RTD green tea (FDC ID 171917) is the closest approved match and is nutritionally consistent with the product formulation. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact Ayataka product label was not directly verified from an approved database entry.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero: unsweetened brewed green tea contains no protein by nature. 2. fat_g is naturally zero: unsweetened brewed green tea contains no fat by nature. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are naturally zero for this beverage. 4. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 171917 for unsweetened RTD green tea. 5. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as brewed tea contains no dietary fibre. 6. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero for a plant-based brewed beverage. 7. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per University of Sydney GI Database convention for plain unsweetened tea. 8. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because available carbohydrate per 300ml serving is 0.6g, yielding GL = (1 × 0.6) / 100 = 0.006, rounded to 0.0. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FDC 171917 for this beverage. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 11. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 19. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 20. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 21. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 22. Input cuisine tag 'Fried Chicken' is a restaurant context tag (Wingstop), not the cuisine of the beverage itself; cuisine corrected to 'Japanese' reflecting the product origin. 23. The brand name 'Authentic Tea House' is the English marketing name used by Coca-Cola Japan for the Ayataka product line in international markets.
